column1: DEBBY 5 AM | No changes. Debby is slowly strengthening with 50 mph winds. No changes to our forecast with the core of the storm staying well west of our coast. Showers and storms pick up in coverage later this morning. Localized flooding is possible with a Flood Watch for the entire area. A tornado threat will increase later today as well.
Thanks for the updates. I share them with my daughter in Philly so she doesn't worry so much about us.
Thankful for you Denis.
Still wishing this storm had gone to Dallas. It would’ve been a great headline.
Thanking Debby and Denis for keeping us out of the cone of certainty.
uncertainty! UN!
Can you spell sense of humor?
Thank you. You keep us so well informed. So appreciate it.
last night he said inland areas did not have to worry. Orlando area is way outside the cone. Probably regular summer rain there.
how about Tampa?
wind and rain event. Along shore or areas that usually flood may see flooding.
thank you!
I’m heading Kissimmee area. Is this for Tampa Area? Or all of FL. I’m worried about the flooding. He mentions localized flooding.
https://forecast.weather.gov/MapClick.php?lat=28.2947...
during heavy rain events there is always localized flooding in low lying areas. Obviously the worst flooding will be on the coast.

Watching from JAX, very concerned about flooding due to the slooooowwww speed 🌀 Our neighbors in SE Georgia are watching and waiting, also. Praying it speeds UP and when it goes into the Atlantic, if fizzles instead of doubling back! We've have those hurricanes before - No bueno! 🙄
Good morning! Thanks for the update. I live in St. Pete at 49th/22nd and it’s beautiful out - somewhat windy and cooler - and I appreciate your update. 🌬
Thx Dennis. Your forecasts are the very best! Thx for all U do. I cashier at the Crystal Beach Publix. Were U at my store yesterday for your Little Debbie’s???
he may have been. He doesn't live to far from there.
No changes? It looks like the projected intensity has increased and the timing of said intensity also increased (hurricane strength right at/after it passes Tampa Bay).

It's making the turn so much earlier than expected. It's moving NNW now and I'm sure N very soon. Bottom of cone maybe even a bit below. Tampa better not just take it lightly we in for some bad weather today and tomorrow! Stay safe!

We’re not local to FL and planned on going to Disney today for a birthday celebration. We know nothing about hurricanes and what to expect - with what you know about today’s anticipated storm, would you still go?

A few years ago, that’s exactly what we did. We live in Tampa and the storm was making landfall South in Fort Myers. We decided to go to Disney because schools in Tampa area were closed to serve as emergency shelters.
It rained at Disney, but IT WASN’T BUSY!! We made it a day trip, and returned to Tampa that night. After the storm made landfall, it actually headed NE from F Myers to Orlando and Disney closed the following day.
Calm, before the storm...
